message.content:
You are assessing one Coachella Valley dining establishment for an honest category-map artifact. The artifact's job is to map who's there, who wins what, and where the visibility gaps are.

Entity: Johnny Costa's Ristorante (Palm Springs)
Cuisine/type signal: White-tablecloth / classic Italian; Sinatra-era institution
Why it surfaced in discovery: Founder Johnny Costa (Naples-born) was Frank Sinatra's personal chef; operating in Palm Springs since 1976; voted Best Italian by Palm Springs Life readers multiple consecutive years; signature Steak Sinatra and linguine clams still on the menu; now run by son Vince Costa.
Discovery sources: https://johnnycostaspalmsprings.com/history/ | https://pstribune.com/2023/01/08/johnny-costas-history-with-frank-sinatra-and-fine-italian-cuisine-in-palm-springs/ | https://www.palmspringslife.com/restaurants/7-must-try-italian-restaurants-across-greater-palm-springs/

Verify the entity exists, then assess on four dimensions. Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") to investigate. Read the entity's own website. Read 2-3 review-platform pages. Run regional queries to test visibility.

REPUTATION:
- review_volume — approximate Google/Yelp review count band (e.g., "3000+", "500-1000", "<100"). Mark [UNVERIFIED] if you can't find it.
- sentiment — current sentiment summary (positive / mostly positive / mixed / declining / negative). Recent reviews weighted over stale.
- recency_signal — what do reviews in the last 6-12 months say?
- longevity — years operating, ownership stability. Mark [UNVERIFIED] if unclear.
- credentials — earned third-party recognition (James Beard nominee/winner, Michelin, Food Network, Yelp Top 100, OpenTable Top, Desert Sun / Palm Springs Life awards, local press features). Empty array if none. Do NOT invent credentials.

AGENT_VISIBILITY:
- appears_in_queries — which regional queries actually surface this entity? Test specific queries: "best restaurants Coachella Valley", "fine dining Palm Desert", "[entity's cuisine] near Palm Springs", "date night Rancho Mirage", "best breakfast [city]".
- description_accuracy — when it surfaces, is the description correct?
- site_structured_data — fetch the entity's own website. Does it carry schema.org markup, clean hours, menu structure, location signals an agent can read?

VISIBILITY GAP — score 0-10. 0 = reputation and visibility aligned. 10 = high reputation, low/wrong visibility.

RELATIONSHIPS:
- competitors — 2-4 direct competitors in same city or cuisine bracket
- corridor_role — anchor / supporting / outlier. Major corridors: El Paseo (Palm Desert), Old Town La Quinta, downtown Palm Springs, The River (Rancho Mirage), festival-adjacent Indio, north Palm Desert development zone, uptown design district Palm Springs.
- query_wins — which queries it actually dominates

CONVERTIBILITY (the layer above the map):
- tier — "anchor-reference" (chain/franchise/resort-owned, no reachable local DM), "convertible" (independently owned, reachable, evidence of marketing spend), or "unclear"
- ownership_signal — independent / chain / franchise / resort-owned / hotel-restaurant / unclear
- marketing_spend_signal — what evidence of marketing budget? real site / paid listings / agency footprint / active social
- reasoning — one paragraph

sources_consulted: list URLs you actually consulted.
flags: anything you couldn't verify — list the unverified claim with [UNVERIFIED] tag.

Stay disciplined: do NOT invent awards, credentials, history, or specific stats. If you can't verify, flag it. The downstream verification layer will catch hallucinations.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

content.content:
# Johnny Costa's Ristorante - Key Information

**History & Founding:**
Johnny Costa's Ristorante was established circa 1976 in Desert Hot Springs, then relocated to Cathedral City before settling at its current Palm Springs location. The restaurant was founded by Johnny Costa, an Italian chef born in Naples.

**Ownership:**
The establishment remains family-owned and operated. Johnny Costa's son, Vince Costa, serves as Head Chef and continues the family culinary traditions, with his brothers and cousins assisting in restaurant operations.

**Sinatra Connection:**
Frank Sinatra was a regular patron. Johnny Costa prepared two signature dishes for Sinatra that remain menu staples: Steak Sinatra and linguine clams. Costa initially met Sinatra while working as Sous Chef at Villa Capri in Hollywood during the 1960s, later working as Head Chef at Martony's where Sinatra and other celebrities dined.

**Credentials & Awards:**
The restaurant has received recognition from OpenTable (2025 badge displayed) and won a "Best of the Best" award (referenced by 2020 badge).

**Additional Context:**
The venue is described as "__'Family owned and operated. Experience Costa's authentic Italian cuisine.'__" The restaurant attracts both celebrities and local patrons seeking classic Italian dining in a nostalgic atmosphere.

Here's a summary of Johnny Costa's Ristorante reviews from Yelp, Google, and other review platforms:

## Overall Reception

Johnny Costa's is a Palm Springs staple renowned for its "consistently great food" and "impeccable service." The restaurant has consistently high ratings across multiple platforms.

## Key Highlights

**Food Quality:** Reviewers praise "an absolutely amazing meal with the best service" and highlight dishes like "penne vodka was outstanding." The upscale Italian restaurant features authentic dishes like lasagna, eggplant Parmesan, and veal piccata.

**Service:** Food and service are "always top notch," with diners who "dine here regularly and are never disappointed."

**Signature Dishes:** Popular favorites include the Angel Hair Shrimp Pomodoro, Linguini with Clams, Steak Sinatra, Lasagna, and Ravioli.

**Historic Background:** Frank Sinatra was once a regular at this fine Italian restaurant, which has been honored by Palm Springs Life as best Italian restaurant for 10 consecutive years. The Costa family continues the legacy of late chef-owner Johnny Costa, who was pals with Sinatra and later served as the singer's personal chef at his desert home.

**Atmosphere:** The nostalgic atmosphere at Johnny Costa's Ristorante feels like traveling back to midcentury times, with classic Italian music and candlelit tables.

content.content:
# Johnny Costa's Ristorante - Key Metrics

**Review Count:** 650 reviews

**Rating:** 4.4 of 5 bubbles

**Ranking:** #15 of 281 restaurants in Palm Springs

**Recent Review Themes:**

- **Food Quality:** Mixed feedback with praise for signature dishes like veal piccata, clams and linguine, and tiramisu, but some found certain pasta dishes oversauced or inconsistent
- **Service:** Generally positive, with mentions of "friendly" and "attentive" staff, though one reviewer noted rushed pacing
- **Atmosphere:** Described as nostalgic mid-century ambiance with "old world NYC" charm; some reviewers appreciated the Frank Sinatra connection
- **Value:** Characterized as expensive but reasonable for quality delivered; one reviewer felt it was "overrated" and "below expectations"
- **Consistency:** Some diners reported excellent experiences while others felt dishes were merely "above average, but not great"

Based on the search results, here are some of the best Italian restaurants in Palm Springs and the Coachella Valley in 2025:

## 2025 Award Winners

Palm Springs Life held their annual Best of the Best competition where readers voted for their favorite businesses across Greater Palm Springs, and the 2025 winners for Best Italian Cuisine have been announced.

## Highly Recommended Restaurants

**Johnny Costa's Ristorante** - The restaurant's late founder, Johnny Costa, served as Frank Sinatra's personal chef, and the menu still includes the crooner's favorite Steak Sinatra.

**Kiki's** - Everything at Kiki's is Instagram-ready, from the white plates with dark green accents to the seasonal cuisine, including homemade focaccia, panzanella (with Coachella dates), sugar snap pea pizza, and short ribs with polenta.

**Il Corso** - For fine Italian dining, Il Corso has a gorgeous modern design that'll transport you straight to a Sicilian villa as you sip on fine wines, Italian-style martinis and decadent dishes.

**Sammy G's** - Located in downtown Palm Springs, Sammy G's merges traditional Italian cuisine with California flair, and the wild mushroom ravioli is one of their most popular dishes.

**Riccio's** - Riccio's has been serving Italian cuisine in Palm Springs for over 30 years, with a patio that has a great view of downtown Palm Springs and a more elegant and cozy interior.

# Schema.org & Structured Data Analysis

Based on the provided HTML content, **no JSON-LD or schema.org markup is present**. There are no `<script type="application/ld+json">` blocks, `itemtype` attributes, or microdata visible in the source.

## Open Graph Tags

Similarly, **no Open Graph meta tags appear** in the document provided. This means the restaurant's website lacks structured data for search engines and social media platforms.

## Implications

The absence of schema.org markup means:
- Search engines cannot automatically extract key details (address, hours, phone number)
- Social media sharing won't display rich previews
- Local SEO performance is diminished
- Voice assistants cannot easily access business information

While the HTML contains human-readable contact information ("440 S. Palm Canyon Drive," hours listed as "Tuesday to Thursday 5 PM– 9:30 PM"), this data isn't machine-readable without structured markup.

Based on the search results, here are some excellent fine dining recommendations for a date night in Palm Springs:

## Top Fine Dining Recommendations

**Mister Parker's** - Perfect for date night, anniversaries, and special occasions where you can put on your finest attire. This is a Palm Springs fine dining destination known as the most romantic restaurant in the area, with an epicurean menu and an impressive wine list. Jackets and collared shirts are required for gentlemen.

**4 Saints** - Choose this restaurant for a sultry date-night ambiance, with shareable small plates like short rib ravioli, corn agnolotti, and grilled Spanish octopus.

**FARM** - A traditional Provençal-style restaurant in downtown Palm Springs with a menu of traditional European dishes using locally sourced produce, where the prix fixe dinner menu is served at a leisurely pace while you enjoy the ambiance of being transported to the South of France.

**The Tropicale** - With an old-school Miami supper club feel, this is a fine dining experience with an impressive cocktail list and back patio, perfect for a grown-up, kitschy oasis.

**Bar Cecil** - One of the trendy Palm Springs spots that's totally worth the visit if you manage to get a booking.

**Copley's** - An award-winning restaurant serving American cuisine with tropical and European flavors, located in part of the former Cary Grant estate with upscale dining either on the patio with mountain views or indoors.

All of these restaurants are known for their romantic atmospheres and quality cuisine, making them excellent choices for a memorable date night in Palm Springs.

Discover the best Italian Restaurants in Palm Springs","url":"https://visitpalmsprings.com/blog/post/the-best-pizza-and-italian-food-in-palm-springs/"},{"title":"30 Best Italian Restaurants In Palm Springs ...","url":"https://www.opentable.com/cuisine/best-italian-restaurants-palm-springs-ca"},{"title":"Il Giardino Italian Restaurant - Palm Springs, CA","url":"https://ilgiardinopalmsprings.com/"},{"title":"That’s Amore! | Palm Springs Life","url":"https://www.palmspringslife.com/restaurants/italian-food-palm-springs/"},{"title":"Old school Italian - dining w/mobsters - Review of Castelli's, Palm Desert, CA - Tripadvisor","url":"https://www.tripadvisor.com/ShowUserReviews-g32846-d448004-r184685362-Castelli_s-Palm_Desert_Greater_Palm_Springs_California.html"},{"title":"Johnny Costa's Restaurant - Palm Springs, CA | OpenTable","url":"https://www.opentable.com/r/johnny-costas-palm-springs-4"},{"title":"Palm Springs Italian Restaurants | Vacation Palm Springs","url":"https://vacationpalmsprings.com/restaurants/italian"},{"title":"The 21 best Italian restaurants in Palm Springs","url":"https://wanderlog.com/list/geoCategory/54322/best-italian-restaurants-in-palm-springs"}]

Based on the search results, here are some classic old school Italian restaurants in Palm Springs near South Palm Canyon:

## Cipolline Osteria
Located at 1260 S Palm Canyon Dr, this Italian dinner house features rib-eyes, salads, homemade pasta, Neapolitan style pizza, Italian wine, and a full bar with a cozy old school vibe.

## Johnny Costa's Ristorante
From the classic Italian music to the candlelit tables, the nostalgic atmosphere at Johnny Costa's Ristorante feels like traveling back to midcentury times. The restaurant has been honored by Palm Springs Life as best Italian restaurant for 10 consecutive years. The Costa family continues the legacy of late chef-owner Johnny Costa, who was pals with Sinatra when he moved to Hollywood from New York in the 1960s, and a decade later served as the singer's personal chef at his desert home.

## Kalura Trattoria
Located at 265 S Palm Canyon Drive, Il Giardino offers Sicilian-inspired comfort food and is one of the best Italian restaurants in Palm Springs for handmade pasta and classic Sicilian desserts. Note: The search also mentions Kalura Trattoria at 124 S Palm Canyon Drive with classic fare and cocktails.

These establishments capture the classic old school Italian dining experience that characterizes traditional Palm Springs dining culture.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Sinatra and historic dining in Palm Springs:

## Melvyn's Restaurant

Melvyn's Restaurant in Palm Springs opened in 1975 at Ingleside Inn and quickly became a hotspot for scores of celebrities, not the least of which was Frank Sinatra. Frank Sinatra became one of its most loyal patrons, making Table 13 his own, and he famously held his rehearsal dinner there in 1976 before marrying Barbara Marx.

Melvyn's Restaurant was designed as an elegant dining venue that harkened back to the classic supper clubs of the 1930s and 1940s. The menu's tableside classics—Steak Diane, Cherries Jubilee, Bananas Foster—became signature rituals, as tuxedoed waiters lit flambés that dazzled guests.

The guest list over the decades includes Elizabeth Taylor, Liza Minnelli, Lucille Ball, Debbie Reynolds, John Travolta, Goldie Hawn, Kurt Russell, and David Hasselhoff, among many others. Salvador Dali, Spencer Tracy, and Dinah Shore were regular guests.

## Other Sinatra Dining Spots

Johnny Costa's Ristorante was founded by Chef Johnny Costa, a Naples-born culinary legend and personal chef to Frank Sinatra. When Costa moved to Palm Springs, Sinatra often joined him at Club Trinidad and later at Johnny's own restaurant.

reputation.review_volume: Yelp 599 reviews; Tripadvisor 650 reviews; Google rating ~4.6 (exact Google count [UNVERIFIED] — secondary sources cite 496–539 reviewers); OpenTable cited at ~2575 diner reviews in one source [UNVERIFIED specific number]
reputation.sentiment: mostly positive — strong fan base praises nostalgic atmosphere, signature dishes (Steak Sinatra, linguine clams, veal piccata), and attentive service; minority of recent reviews flag inconsistency, oversauced pasta, or 'overrated' value
reputation.recency_signal: Recent (2025–2026) reviews on Tripadvisor and Yelp continue to characterize it as a Palm Springs institution with strong service and signature dishes intact; some mixed notes on consistency and price-to-value
reputation.longevity: Operating since circa 1976 (Desert Hot Springs → Cathedral City → current Palm Springs location at 440 S. Palm Canyon Dr.); family-owned with son Vince Costa as Head Chef — ~50 years, stable family ownership
credentials: Palm Springs Life Readers' Choice — 'Best Italian Restaurant' (multiple consecutive years; site/press references '10 consecutive years' [UNVERIFIED exact count])
OpenTable 2025 recognition badge displayed on site
Featured in Palm Springs Life '7 Must-Try Italian Restaurants Across Greater Palm Springs'
Featured in Visit Greater Palm Springs official tourism listings
Featured in regional press (PS Tribune) for Sinatra legacy

agent_visibility.appears_in_queries: Surfaces reliably for: 'best Italian restaurant Palm Springs', 'classic/old-school Italian Palm Springs', 'Sinatra restaurants Palm Springs', 'historic Palm Springs dining'. Does NOT consistently surface for generic 'date night Palm Springs' or 'fine dining Palm Springs' queries — those are dominated by Mister Parker's, 4 Saints, FARM, Bar Cecil, Copley's, The Tropicale. Weak presence in broader 'best restaurants Coachella Valley' queries despite tenure.
agent_visibility.description_accuracy: When surfaced, descriptions are generally accurate (Sinatra/personal-chef framing, signature dishes, midcentury nostalgia) — though the 'personal chef to Sinatra' framing appears stronger in derivative sources than on the restaurant's own history page, which describes the relationship as developing from Costa's Hollywood chef roles. The '10 consecutive years' Palm Springs Life claim circulates without clear primary citation.
agent_visibility.site_structured_data: Weak. Own website (johnnycostaspalmsprings.com) shows clean human-readable NAP (address 440 S. Palm Canyon Dr, phone 760-325-4556, hours Tues–Thurs 5–9:30, Fri–Sat 5–10, Sun–Mon closed) and dedicated menu/wine/dessert/drink pages — but no detectable JSON-LD schema.org markup, no Restaurant schema, no Open Graph tags observed in fetched HTML. Machine-readability for agents is minimal; relies on third-party aggregators (OpenTable, Tripadvisor, Yelp, Visit Greater Palm Springs) to broker structured signals.

competitors: Cipolline Osteria (1260 S Palm Canyon Dr — direct corridor neighbor, classic Italian dinner house)
Sammy G's Tuscan Grill (downtown Palm Springs)
Melvyn's at Ingleside (adjacent Sinatra-era institution — not Italian but competes on legacy/old-school clientele)
relationships.corridor_role: anchor — long-tenured legacy anchor on the South Palm Canyon Drive stretch of downtown Palm Springs; one of the corridor's defining Sinatra-era dining identities alongside Melvyn's

convertibility.ownership_signal: independent — family-owned and operated by the Costa family (son Vince Costa as Head Chef, brothers/cousins involved); no chain, franchise, or hotel parent
convertibility.marketing_spend_signal: Owned domain with multi-page site (menu, wine, drinks, history, desserts); active OpenTable presence with paid badge; listed on Visit Greater Palm Springs (tourism partnership); featured editorially in Palm Springs Life; no obvious agency/SEO footprint and no schema markup, suggesting marketing spend skews toward reservations platforms and legacy reputation rather than digital/agent optimization
convertibility.reasoning: Independent, family-owned, ~50-year operator with a reachable owner-operator (Vince Costa) and an evident but lightweight digital footprint. Marketing budget appears modest and concentrated on OpenTable + reservation platforms; the website lacks schema.org markup, Open Graph tags, and structured menu/hours data — meaning agent-layer visibility is mediated almost entirely through third parties. High reputation + thin machine-readable surface = textbook convertible candidate for an agent-readiness uplift.
